inline_comments.message: How the released image tag does not include the timestamp and is only X.Y.Z ? Is it what we are going to have for the release ?

This is for Istanbul  onwards. As you mentioned yourself in the JIRA, it was not a blocker for H release. When we release the <version>.yaml file this timestamp containing image is retagged/released as per the yaml filename. Correct me if I got you question wrong.

Yes, my question was not for Honolulu, it was for the next release we are going to make, then Istanbul.

I wanted to ensure that the release job is not going to tag the release image with the timestamp 1.1.0-yyyyMMdd'T'HHmmss'Z' instead of only 1.1.0.

I'm not sure how the release job behave to handle the timestamp. Even if ${maven.build.timestamp} property is unset, it sounds to be that we are going to end up with a remaining "-" after the version number for the release.

This is what I was wondering, just by looking at the change.
